summary: Large matching/decompilation PR for src/melee/ty/tydisplay.c, replacing many placeholder stubs with implemented tyDisplay routines and adding local structs/types for display grid/config/archive/background data. The PR also adjusted related call sites and headers: Ground_801C5878 now passes tyDisplay_8031C2EC's result into un_8031C454, it_8027CC88 uses a correctly typed s32 stack array and cleaner un_8031C354 pointer arguments, and toy.h gives un_80305D00 a concrete float-returning prototype. Review focused on replacing raw __assert patterns with HSD_ASSERT and on preserving OSPanic string/file handling in a way that helps future matching/linking.
what_changed: Implemented a large block of previously stubbed tyDisplay functions in src/melee/ty/tydisplay.c, including un_8031830C, un_80318714, un_80318B1C, un_80318CB4, un_80319540, and many later functions indicated by the diff replacing placeholder comments.

Added many dependencies to tydisplay.c, including db, text drawing, lb archive/audio/vector helpers, mnmain, Dolphin OS, baselib archive/cobj/debug/fog/gobj/jobj/lobj/memory, and MSL math/trig headers.

Introduced local tyDisplay-related data structures in tydisplay.c such as TyDspBaseData, TyDspArchiveHolder, TyDspBgData, TyDspArchNames, TyDspNameTables, TyDspSceneGfx, TyDspPos, TyDspGrid, and TyDspConfig.

Added extern declarations for tyDisplay globals and static data such as un_804D6F10, un_804D6F14, un_804D6F18, un_804D6F1C, un_804D6F20, un_804D6F24, un_804A2DE8, un_804A2D98, un_803B8988, and un_803B8A34.
Implemented sorting logic through an inline quicksort helper used by both un_8031830C and un_80318714, with recursive calls matching the symbol names used by the binary.
Implemented tyDisplay layout-generation logic visible in the excerpt: circular/ring positioning with trig/random perturbation in un_80318CB4 and grid/row-column positioning in un_80319540.
Updated Ground_801C5878 so un_8031C454 receives temp_r30, the value returned by tyDisplay_8031C2EC, before storing it in stage_info.x6E4[0].
Changed it_8027CC88's local sp1C buffer from u32[10] to s32[10] and simplified un_8031C354 calls to pass sp1C and &sp1C[temp_r3] directly rather than casts to s32(*)[].
Changed toy.h prototype for un_80305D00 from unknown return/params to float un_80305D00(void).
Updated tydisplay.h, ty/types.h, and tylist.h with additional or corrected declarations/types, though the excerpt only shows their file-level change counts.
smart_moves: Kept duplicated sorting entry points un_8031830C and un_80318714 as separate exported functions while sharing implementation through an inline quicksort helper, preserving symbol-level behavior without duplicating all logic.
Used explicit layout structs with offset comments for tyDisplay state, which makes future matching work around archive holders, scene graphics, grids, and config fields easier to audit.
Typed the it_8027CC88 stack buffer as s32, allowing cleaner pointer arithmetic and removing noisy casts around un_8031C354 calls.
Converted a raw assert pattern in the visible diff to HSD_ASSERT(0xC6, 0), matching reviewer guidance and project conventions for baselib debug assertions.
Ground call-site fix suggests the decompilation clarified un_8031C454's parameter contract: the selected/display id from tyDisplay_8031C2EC is an input, not just a side-effect-free preceding call.
decomp_lessons: When an assert in decompiled code appears as __assert("file", line, "0"), prefer the project's HSD_ASSERT(line, 0) form when that matches the intended baselib assertion path.
For OSPanic-like sites, reviewers prefer using __FILE__ for the filename and declaring the actual panic string when needed; this can make later matching and linking easier.
Do not leave one-off static strings above a function if they can be used directly in the actual call; unnecessary static declarations can make matching/linking harder.
If a stack array is semantically signed, type it as s32 rather than u32 and avoid function-pointer/array casts; cleaner types can improve both readability and match stability.

Wrapper functions can preserve multiple binary entry points while reusing one inline implementation, but recursive calls may still need to name the real exported symbols to match generated control flow.

For data-heavy UI/display code, local structs with offset-accurate padding are useful until symbols and shared headers mature; keep names descriptive enough to be searchable but avoid overclaiming unknown fields.
naming_conventions: Local tyDisplay structs use the TyDsp prefix: TyDspBaseData, TyDspArchiveHolder, TyDspBgData, TyDspArchNames, TyDspNameTables, TyDspSceneGfx, TyDspPos, TyDspGrid, TyDspConfig.
Unknown struct fields are named by offset or rough role, such as x04_min_x, x08_min_z, x0C_max_x, x10_max_z, x75, x78, pad_04, pad_FC, and pad_106.
Function names largely remain address-based/unresolved, such as un_8031830C, un_80318714, un_80318B1C, un_80318CB4, un_80319540, un_8031C354, and un_8031C454.
Existing mixed naming is preserved for exported tyDisplay functions with partial semantic prefixes, such as tyDisplay_8031C2CC and tyDisplay_8031C2EC.
Array/table fields use descriptive plural names where evidence is clear, such as jobj_names, matanim_names, arch_names, entries, pos, and sort.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK in implemented functions, including PAD_STACK(16), PAD_STACK(0x50), and PAD_STACK(0x28), to preserve stack layout for matching.
Used an inline quicksort helper with explicit temporary swaps, pivot selection from midpoints, and recursive calls through un_8031830C/un_80318714 to match the original control flow.
Used bit-level comparison syntax like *(s32*) &sort[n].val in the visible sorting logic, implying a match-driven treatment of values rather than a purely semantic typed comparison.
Kept gotos/retry-style control flow in collision-resolution logic, which is often necessary for matching decompiled loops with non-structured original flow.
Used concrete constants from the binary-visible behavior, including 0x125, 0x124, 0x12E4, 9.0f, 11.0f, 3.5f, 0.017453292f, 360.0f, and 2.1474836e9f.
Reviewer feedback indicates assert and panic spelling/file/string choices affect matching and future linking, not just style.

review_feedback.feedback: Use HSD_ASSERT instead of raw __assert for an assert whose string is just condition "0".
Apply the same HSD_ASSERT replacement at another similar assert site.
For an OSPanic site, replace the filename with __FILE__ and declare the actual string because it makes matching and linking the file later easier.
A string/static should be used directly in the actual function call rather than declared as a separate static above when unnecessary.

follow_up_queries: Inspect final merged src/melee/ty/tydisplay.c to identify which additional placeholder functions beyond the excerpt were implemented and whether any remain stubbed.
Check src/melee/ty/tydisplay.h and src/melee/ty/types.h final declarations to see which TyDsp or TySortElem types should move from local C definitions into shared headers.
Review all assert and OSPanic sites in tydisplay.c for consistency with HSD_ASSERT, __FILE__, and declared panic-string conventions.
Search uses of un_8031C354 and un_8031C454 to confirm their inferred signatures and whether they can be renamed or better typed.
Verify whether un_80305D00's float void prototype enabled additional toy/tydisplay matches or should be propagated to call sites.
